perturbation.original_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Application Software and all documentation and related materials are proprietary to Company and are confidential and constitute a valuable asset of Company, and that the data files contained in the Confidential Information are proprietary to Client and are confidential and constitute a valuable asset of Client. Recipient agrees to safeguard the Confidential Information, and Recipient shall not disclose or give access to the Confidential Information to any person or entity other than those employees of Recipient who have a need for such access in order to assist Client with Conversion.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Application Software and all documentation and related materials are proprietary to Company and are confidential and constitute a valuable asset of Company. Recipient agrees to safeguard the Confidential Information, and Recipient shall not disclose or give access to the Confidential Information to any person or entity other than those employees of Recipient who have a need for such access in order to assist Client with Conversion.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the explicit statement that "the data files contained in the Confidential Information are proprietary to Client and are confidential and constitute a valuable asset of Client," the obligation to protect client data is weakened. This creates an in-text contradiction because later in Section 5, the contract implies a right of the Client to seek damages for unauthorized disclosure of the data. However, the explicit declaration of ownership and confidentiality of the Client's data is now omitted. There is now an in-text contradiction because the clause regarding data protection is weakened by the fact that there isn't anything explicit about the fact that data files contained in the Confidential Information are proprietary to Client and are confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledges that the use or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Application Software by Recipient (including any of its employees or anyone who obtains the Confidential Information or Application Software or gains access thereto from or through Recipient or any of its employees) in a manner inconsistent with this Agreement will cause Company or Client as the case may be, irreparable damage. In such event, Company and Client shall have the right to equitable and injunctive relief to prevent any unauthorized use or disclosure, and to such damages as are occasioned by any such unauthorized use or disclosure, including but not limited to reasonable attorneys' fees and costs incurred in enforcing Company's or Client's rights hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the data files are proprietary to the client. The changed text removes this explicit statement. The contradicted text gives the Client the right to equitable and injunctive relief to prevent any unauthorized use or disclosure. By removing the explicit statement of ownership, the changed text creates an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text because the basis for the client's right to seek damages is weakened, leading to uncertainty about the enforceability of the clause.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall not make any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information and Recipient shall promptly advise Company and Client in writing if Recipient learns of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Application Software by anyone, whether an employee, former employee or agent of Recipient, or others, and shall immediately take all reasonable steps within Recipient's power to stop any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Application Software by anyone. Recipient shall not, and it will not permit anyone else, to copy the Confidential Information or Application Software.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall not make any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information. Recipient shall not, and it will not permit anyone else, to copy the Confidential Information or Application Software.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for the Recipient to "promptly advise Company and Client in writing if Recipient learns of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Application Software by anyone, whether an employee, former employee or agent of Recipient, or others, and shall immediately take all reasonable steps within Recipient's power to stop any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Application Software by anyone," the clause contradicts the purpose of the agreement. The Agreement's purpose is data protection, outlined at the start of the document, however this is now contradicted by removing the disclosure to Company and Client, should the recipient learn of any unauthorized use or disclosure. This affects the overall agreement, since the agreement is primarily for data protection, and now the Recipient isn't obligated to report unauthorized uses.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
THIS CONFIDENTIALITY/NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT PERMITTING ACCESS TO SYSTEM DOCUMENTATION AND DATA FILES FOR DATA CONVERSION (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement") is made and entered into as of the last day and year written below by and between Phoenix International Ltd., Inc., a Florida Corporation, of 900 Winderley Place, Suite 140, Maitland, Florida 32751 ("Company"), and the following parties: CLIENT: RECIPIENT: ------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------- Contact: Contact: ----------------------------------- ----------------------------------------------- Telephone: Telephone: --------------------------------- --------------------------------------------- (above party is hereinafter to as "Client") (above party is hereinafter referred to as "Recipient")
Purpose of Agreement: Company has developed, owns, uses, or re-markets certain confidential and proprietary computer software and related documentation and materials (all of which shall collectively be referred to hereinafter as "Application Software"), which Application Software is used to process certain of Client's data pursuant to a separate license or service agreement between Company and Client. Client desires that certain of its data being processed by the Application Software be converted to another software program (hereinafter referred to as the "Conversion"), and Client desires that Recipient assist with this Conversion. In order for Recipient to perform Recipient's job functions with Client, Client desires that Recipient have access to those data files of Client and to those portions of the documentation for the Application Software which are specifically identified in Paragraph 1 below (those items identified in Paragraph 1 shall collectively be hereinafter referred to as the "Confidential Information"). Recipient, on its own behalf and on behalf of its employees, agrees to abide by the terms of this Agreement. Company and Client agree to permit Recipient to have access to the Confidential Information as requested herein by Client, but only in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Therefore, in consideration of the premises hereof, and other good and valuable consideration not herein recited but the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties hereto agree as follows:
perturbation.justification: The original text placed an obligation on the recipient to report any unauthorized use or disclosure of confidential information. The changed text removes this obligation. The contradicted text states the purpose of the agreement is to protect confidential data. By removing the obligation to report unauthorized use, the changed text directly contradicts the agreement's stated purpose of data protection, as the company and client are no longer guaranteed to be informed of breaches, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event an employee of Recipient terminates his or her employment with Recipient, Recipient agrees to require such terminated employee to immediately return to Recipient all copies of the Confidential Information in such employee's possession at the time of termination of employment. Recipient shall, upon the earlier occurrence of (i) completion of the tasks assigned to it by Client which require access to the Confidential
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall, upon the earlier occurrence of (i) completion of the tasks assigned to it by Client which require access to the Confidential
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for the Recipient to require their terminated employee to immediately return all copies of the confidential information, Recipient is no longer held responsible to requiring copies of the confidential information back from terminated employees. This contradicts section 2, which states that 'Recipient agrees to safeguard the Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Application Software and all documentation and related materials are proprietary to Company and are confidential and constitute a valuable asset of Company, and that the data files contained in the Confidential Information are proprietary to Client and are confidential and constitute a valuable asset of Client. Recipient agrees to safeguard the Confidential Information, and Recipient shall not disclose or give access to the Confidential Information to any person or entity other than those employees of Recipient who have a need for such access in order to assist Client with Conversion.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the Recipient to ensure terminated employees return all confidential information. The changed text omits this requirement. Section 2 states 'Recipient agrees to safeguard the Confidential Information'. By removing the obligation to retrieve confidential information from terminated employees, the Recipient is no longer safeguarding the information as thoroughly, which directly contradicts the existing obligation to safeguard the confidential information. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement DOES NOT grant to Recipient the right to have access to any portion of the Application Software other than the documentation specifically set forth below. The Confidential Information to be disclosed is as follows: Client data files to be Deconverted: ----------------------------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- Application Software Documentation to be Provided to Recipient: -------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement DOES NOT grant to Recipient the right to have access to any portion of the Application Software other than the documentation specifically set forth below.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the line containing "The Confidential Information to be disclosed is as follows: Client data files to be Deconverted: ... Application Software Documentation to be Provided to Recipient: ...", the agreement no longer specifies the exact data and documentation that constitutes the Confidential Information. This creates an internal contradiction with later clauses (Section 3 and 5) that discuss the Recipient's obligations regarding the Confidential Information, since it's now undefined. This is an in-text contradiction because while other areas of the contract allude to 'confidential information', it's not exactly declared here. Therefore, no exact obligation exists.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall not make any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information and Recipient shall promptly advise Company and Client in writing if Recipient learns of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Application Software by anyone, whether an employee, former employee or agent of Recipient, or others, and shall immediately take all reasonable steps within Recipient's power to stop any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Application Software by anyone. Recipient shall not, and it will not permit anyone else, to copy the Confidential Information or Application Software.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that confidential information included client data files and application software documentation. The changed text removes this specification, leaving the term 'Confidential Information' undefined. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which outlines the Recipient's obligations regarding the Confidential Information, because there is no longer a clear definition of what constitutes this information. Therefore, the Recipient's obligations are ambiguous, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Injunctive Relief. Recipient acknowledges that the use or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Application Software by Recipient (including any of its employees or anyone who obtains the Confidential Information or Application Software or gains access thereto from or through Recipient or any of its employees) in a manner inconsistent with this Agreement will cause Company or Client as the case may be, irreparable damage. In such event, Company and Client shall have the right to equitable and injunctive relief to prevent any unauthorized use or disclosure, and to such damages as are occasioned by any such unauthorized use or disclosure, including but not limited to reasonable attorneys' fees and costs incurred in enforcing Company's or Client's rights hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Injunctive Relief. Recipient acknowledges that the use or disclosure of the Application Software by Recipient (including any of its employees or anyone who obtains the Confidential Information or Application Software or gains access thereto from or through Recipient or any of its employees) in a manner inconsistent with this Agreement will cause Company or Client as the case may be, irreparable damage. In such event, Company and Client shall have the right to equitable and injunctive relief to prevent any unauthorized use or disclosure, and to such damages as are occasioned by any such unauthorized use or disclosure, including but not limited to reasonable attorneys' fees and costs incurred in enforcing Company's or Client's rights hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting "Confidential Information" from the first sentence, it weakens the client's ability to protect their data. Section 2 states that the Recipient must 'safeguard' the confidential information. This section now only states that the Application Software must be protected, weakening the Recipient's obligation to safeguard the client's data. This affects section 2, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledges that the Application Software and all documentation and related materials are proprietary to Company and are confidential and constitute a valuable asset of Company, and that the data files contained in the Confidential Information are proprietary to Client and are confidential and constitute a valuable asset of Client. Recipient agrees to safeguard the Confidential Information, and Recipient shall not disclose or give access to the Confidential Information to any person or entity other than those employees of Recipient who have a need for such access in order to assist Client with Conversion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits "Confidential Information" from the sentence discussing irreparable damage, which weakens the protection afforded to the client's data. The contradicted text explicitly states that the Recipient must 'safeguard' the Confidential Information. The changed text only refers to the Application Software, which contradicts the Recipient's obligation to safeguard the client's data as stated in the contradicted text. This is an Omissions - In Text Contradiction, as per the definition.
